ONEM.definition: Federal body that pays and supervises unemployment benefits and issues the official documents (C4, C3…)

ONSS.definition: Collects social contributions from employers and workers and redistributes them to the social security bodies

CAPAC.meaning: Auxiliary Fund for Payment of Unemployment Benefits (CAPAC/HVW)
CAPAC.definition: Public fund that pays unemployment benefits to people not affiliated with a trade union

CPAS.definition: Municipal body that guarantees everyone a life in line with human dignity (integration income, social and medical aid…)

RIS.meaning: Social Integration Income (RIS, the Belgian minimum income)
RIS.definition: Monthly financial aid paid by the CPAS to people without sufficient resources

AGR.definition: Top-up paid by ONEM to involuntary part-time workers to reach a minimum income

C4.definition: Document issued by the employer at the end of the contract — essential to open a right to unemployment benefits

C1.definition: Form to submit to the payment body to set the benefit rate (single, cohabiting, head of household)

C3.definition: Monthly document on which the benefit recipient notes days worked, illness or holidays before sending it to the payment body

BCE.definition: Federal register that assigns a unique company number to every company and self-employed person active in Belgium

BCSS.definition: Data exchange platform between Belgian social security institutions

FOREM.meaning: Walloon Office for Vocational Training and Employment
FOREM.definition: Public employment and training service in Wallonia — where Walloon jobseekers register

VDAB.meaning: Flemish Service for Employment and Vocational Training
VDAB.definition: Flemish public employment and training service — the Flemish equivalent of the FOREM

ACTIRIS.definition: Public employment service in the Brussels-Capital Region — where Brussels jobseekers register

ADG.meaning: Employment Office of the German-speaking Community
ADG.definition: Public employment service for the German-speaking Community (Eastern Cantons)

INASTI.meaning: National Institute for the Social Security of the Self-Employed (INASTI/RSVZ)
INASTI.definition: Manages the social security of the self-employed (pension, health insurance, family benefits…)

INAMI.meaning: National Institute for Health and Disability Insurance (INAMI/RIZIV)
INAMI.definition: Organises healthcare and benefits insurance through the health insurance funds

SPF.definition: Belgian federal administration (e.g. SPF Employment, SPF Finance, SPF Justice)

SPP.definition: Specialised federal administration — the best known is the SPP Social Integration, which coordinates the CPAS

PIIS.definition: Contract concluded between the CPAS and the RIS beneficiary, setting personalised goals (training, employment, housing…)

DIMONA.definition: Electronic declaration that the employer must send to the ONSS no later than the start of each assignment

DRS.definition: Set of electronic declarations (unemployment, illness, accident…) sent by the employer to the social security bodies

NISS.definition: Unique 11-digit number identifying each person in the Belgian social security system — printed on the back of the ID card

CCT.definition: Agreement signed between employers and trade unions setting working conditions (wages, leave…) for a sector or company

CP.definition: Sectoral body where employers and trade unions negotiate working conditions. Each sector has its own (numbered) joint committee

ASBL.definition: Legal form of association whose profits are not distributed to members but reinvested in its social purpose

AR.definition: Act signed by the King on the proposal of a minister — the main source of regulatory law in Belgium

ALE.definition: Lets a long-term unemployed person carry out occasional work (gardening, childcare…) while keeping their benefits

PFI.definition: In-company training (Wallonia) for a jobseeker: they keep their benefits and receive a bonus from the employer

TVA.definition: Indirect tax paid on most goods and services. Belgian standard rate: 21%

outils.tutCarteStep1Body: Gather your ID card, a recent proof of address, and your national registry number before you begin.
outils.tutCarteStep2Title: Sign in with itsme or eID
outils.tutCarteStep2Body: Identify yourself securely via the itsme app or your eID card reader.
outils.tutCarteStep3Body: Provide the required information: reason for the request, delivery address, payment method.
outils.tutCarteStep4Body: You will receive a receipt and a link to track progress. The card is usually available within 2 to 3 weeks.
outils.tutEboxStep1Body: Sign in once on my.belgium.be with itsme to activate your secure inbox.
outils.tutEboxStep2Body: Select the administrations that may send you their official documents digitally (NEO/ONEM, health insurance, FPS Finance, etc.).
outils.tutEboxStep3Body: Each new document lands directly in your eBox. An email notification is sent every time.
outils.tutMyonemStep1Body: Visit the ONEM portal and start the account creation process via itsme or eID.
outils.tutMyonemStep2Body: Complete your profile: address, household composition, payment institution (CAPAC or union).
outils.tutMyonemStep3Body: From your dashboard, access your forms (C4, C1, C3.2, etc.), already partially pre-filled.
outils.tutMyonemStep4Body: Check the details of each payment, any deductions, and the history of your benefits.
outils.infoAleIntro: A Local Employment Agency (ALE/PWA) allows long-term jobseekers and social welfare recipients to take on activities of public interest, on top of their benefits.
outils.infoAleItem1Body: Fully unemployed people who have been entitled to benefits for at least 2 years (1 year if you are over 45) and people receiving the minimum integration income for at least 6 months.
outils.infoAleItem2Body: Home help, gardening, small maintenance jobs, support of persons, tutoring — always in addition to the normal economic circuit.
outils.infoAleItem3Body: You keep your benefits and receive about EUR 4.10 net per ALE/PWA voucher (1 hour of work). Maximum 45 hours/month, partial tax exemption.
outils.infoAleItem4Body: Contact the ALE/PWA of your municipality. You will sign an ALE/PWA contract and receive your voucher booklet. Registration is free.
outils.infoRisIntro: The Social Integration Income (RIS/leefloon) is financial support from the OCMW/CPAS for people with insufficient income who cannot claim it through another channel.
outils.infoRisItem1Body: Any adult legally residing in Belgium, without sufficient resources, willing to work (except for health or fairness reasons).
outils.infoRisItem2Body: Cohabitant: approx. EUR 911/month. Single: approx. EUR 1,366/month. Person with dependent family: approx. EUR 1,847/month. Regularly indexed.
outils.infoRisItem3Body: At the OCMW/CPAS of your municipality of residence. A social worker will assess your situation and carry out a social investigation within 30 days.
outils.infoRisItem4Body: Willingness to work, individualised social integration project (PIIS), declaration of any income. The RIS can be combined with other aid (energy, housing, medical).
outils.linkActirisDesc: Actiris is the Brussels public employment service. It supports jobseekers in the Brussels-Capital Region in their search, training, and (re)integration into the labour market.
outils.linkVdabDesc: VDAB is the Flemish public employment service. It offers training, individual support, and a job platform for residents of the Flemish Region.
outils.linkForemDesc: Le Forem is the Walloon public employment and training service. It supports Walloon jobseekers and offers a broad catalogue of qualifying training.
outils.linkAdgDesc: ADG (Arbeitsamt der DG) is the public employment service of the German-speaking Community. It supports jobseekers from the Eastern Cantons.

outils.ophTitle: Which payment institution should you choose?
outils.ophIntro: In Belgium, there are 4 possible institutions to receive your unemployment benefits. The choice is yours — here's how to decide.
outils.ophCapacBody: The Auxiliary Fund for the Payment of Unemployment Benefits is the public institution, run by the State. No membership fee, free. Only provides benefit payment — no legal defence or union services.
outils.ophCapacFor: Who is it for? You don't want to join a union, or you're looking for the simplest and most neutral option.
outils.ophSyndicatBody: The 3 Belgian unions also pay benefits to their members and additionally offer legal defence, labour law advice, and support. Monthly membership fee (~15-20 €/month depending on union and status).
outils.ophSyndicatFgtb: FGTB/ABVV (Belgian General Federation of Labour) — socialist, more present in industry
outils.ophSyndicatCsc: CSC/ACV (Confederation of Christian Trade Unions) — largest Belgian union
outils.ophSyndicatSynova: SYNOVA (formerly CGSLB/ACLVB, General Confederation of Liberal Trade Unions of Belgium) — free trade union, smaller
outils.ophSyndicatFor: Who is it for? You want active support (employer dispute, negotiation, advice) on top of the payment.

landing.reformeMetaDesc: Summary of the changes to Belgian unemployment rules in force since 1 March 2026: time limits, integration benefits, access conditions.
landing.reformeIntro: What changed for your benefits since 1 March 2026. A simple summary — to understand your situation and find the right step.
landing.reformeChange1Body: Since 1 March 2026, the right to full unemployment is limited: a base period of 12 months, extendable by up to 12 more months depending on your work history — i.e. 24 months maximum in the general scheme. Exceptions exist (certain end-of-career situations, SWT/RCC, disability…).
landing.reformeChange2Title: Integration benefits are capped at 12 months
landing.reformeChange2Body: For young people who've left school, integration benefits are now limited to 12 months in the general scheme, with extensions possible in certain situations.
landing.reformeChange3Body: For a new full-unemployment claim after employment from 1 March 2026, the ONEM applies the principle of 312 days of salaried and/or equivalent work over a 36-month reference period, regardless of your age.
landing.reformeChange4Title: New claims, transitions and end-of-entitlement are distinguished
landing.reformeChange4Body: A situation already open before 1 March 2026 may fall under transitional measures: it doesn't necessarily follow the same rules as a new claim. If you received a letter from the ONEM or your payment institution, read it carefully.
landing.reformeDisclaimer: This page is informational: it summarises the main points but doesn't calculate your rights or amounts, and doesn't replace the ONEM or your payment institution. When in doubt, contact them.
landing.reformeCtaTitle: Not sure which step applies to you?
landing.reformeCtaSubtitle: Answer a few questions to find the right file.
landing.reformeSource1: ONEM — Reform of the unemployment regulations
landing.reformeSource2: ONEM — New unemployment rules in force since 1 March 2026
landing.reformeSource3: ONEM — Are you entitled to benefits after employment?
landing.bookingShowcaseCtaCaption: Exactly the journey your members will use.
landing.ctParcoursIntro: From the notification to the NEO to the payment of benefits: who does what, in which order — and the deadline pitfall to avoid.
landing.ctParcoursStep1Desc: Identify the suspension reason (economic, bad weather, force majeure…). Depending on the reason, a prior electronic notification to the NEO is required before the start of the period.
landing.ctParcoursStep2Desc: Inform the employees concerned and report the first effective day of unemployment to the NEO — every month when the reason requires it.
landing.ctParcoursStep3Desc: On their side, your employee files their request with their payment institution: the C3.2 is the cornerstone of their file.
landing.ctParcoursStep3WarningMessage: Install the eC3.2 application as soon as the file starts — otherwise retroactive compensation is limited to 1 month.
landing.ctParcoursStep4Desc: The payment institution (trade union or CAPAC) pays the benefits to the employee, based on your electronic declaration (DRS / WECH).
landing.echeancierIntro: Temporary unemployment and end of contract follow a precise rhythm. Here are the recurring appointments not to miss — and ready-made reminders to drop into your calendar.
landing.echeancierCtaNote: 3 recurring monthly reminders, compatible with Outlook, Google Calendar and Apple Calendar.
landing.echeancierObligation1Title: Notify temporary unemployment to the NEO
landing.echeancierObligation1Desc: For economic unemployment and the suspension of white-collar workers, the planned period must be notified to the NEO before the start of the suspension. Bad weather, however, requires no prior notification.
landing.echeancierObligation2Title: Hand the control card to the employee
landing.echeancierObligation2Desc: Every employee concerned receives their control card (C3.2A) from the first effective day of unemployment of the month — it conditions the payment of their benefits.
landing.echeancierObligation3Title: Report the 1st effective day of unemployment
landing.echeancierObligation3Desc: Economic, bad weather, technical breakdown or suspension of white-collar workers: the first effective day of unemployment of the month is reported to the NEO, generally on the same day or one working day around it (variants depending on the reason).
landing.echeancierObligation4Desc: The electronic DRS declaration (WECH stream) is sent at the end of the month via the social security portal: it is what allows the calculation and payment of your employees' benefits.
landing.echeancierObligation5Title: Hand the C4 to the employee
landing.echeancierObligation5Desc: The C4 unemployment certificate attests to the periods of work and the reason for the end of contract. The employee needs it quickly to assert their rights with their payment institution.
landing.echeancierObligation6Desc: Notifications, communications, declarations and handed-over documents: keep a record of them. In case of an audit or dispute, these supporting documents are evidence.
landing.echeancierReminder1Summary: Temporary unemployment: report the 1st effective day to the NEO
landing.echeancierReminder1Description: If temporary unemployment is planned this month: report the first effective day to the NEO and check that every employee has received their control card (C3.2A). Indicative reminder created by Docbel — to be adapted to your situation.
landing.echeancierReminder2Summary: Prepare the declaration of temporary unemployment hours (DRS)
landing.echeancierReminder2Description: Prepare the electronic month-end declaration (DRS / WECH) via the social security portal: it is used for the payment of your employees' benefits. Indicative reminder created by Docbel.
landing.echeancierReminder3Summary: Monthly social closing: declarations sent, supporting documents archived
landing.echeancierReminder3Description: Check that the month's declarations have been sent and archive notifications, communications and supporting documents. Indicative reminder created by Docbel.
landing.echeancierFootnote: Indicative overview: the exact terms (deadlines, channels, forms) depend on the temporary unemployment reason and your situation. When in doubt, refer to the official instructions of the NEO or your payroll office.

sub.help: Temporary unemployment = your contract continues but work is suspended for a while (lack of work, bad weather, force majeure, strike, collective closure…). You will return to the same employer.

ouiCt.label: Yes, I will return to the same employer

nonFin.label: No, my contract has ended for good

perteEmploi.label: I have lost my job
perteEmploi.description: Dismissal, end of contract or end of fixed-term contract

sub.help: We want to know whether you have already worked long enough to open a right, or whether you are just leaving school.

passeTravailBe.label: I have worked in Belgium for a fair while
passeTravailBe.help: You have a salaried work history in Belgium (several months to several years).

refine.question: Is this your very first unemployment claim?
refine.help: « First time » = you have never received unemployment benefits before. If you have ever received any, even long ago, it counts as a renewed claim.

premiere.label: Yes, it is the first time

sorsEtudes.label: I am leaving school and have worked little or not at all

travailEtranger.label: I have worked abroad (EU/EEA)
travailEtranger.help: You worked in a European Union / EEA country and you are returning to Belgium.

refine.question: Have you worked again in Belgium since your return?
refine.help: Working again in Belgium (in practice at least 3 months under the current rule) opens a full Belgian right. Otherwise your file goes through the cross-border regime with the U1 form.

retravailleBe.label: Yes, I have worked again in Belgium

pasRetravaille.label: No, I have just come back from abroad

jeuneEtudes.label: I am leaving school / I am young

vingtCinqPlus.label: 25 or older

refine.question: Have you ever worked (as an employee)?

frontalierClassique.help: You live in Belgium (or a neighbouring country) and you work on the other side of the border, returning home at least once a week.

refine.help: Working again in Belgium (≈ at least 3 months) opens a full Belgian right. Otherwise it is the cross-border regime with the U1 form.

retravailleBe.label: Yes, I have worked again in Belgium

finCarriere.label: I am at the end of my career (dismissed)

ct.title: Temporary unemployment

ct.title: Temporary unemployment
ct.description: Helps you build the temporary unemployment file (economic, bad weather, force majeure, etc.) to submit to ONEM/RVA.

delaiIntroduction.title: Submission deadline
delaiIntroduction.message: The submission deadline varies by claim type: generally current month + 2 months (mandatory) or + 36 months (optional). Some situations (Q/S change, strike, 66+ years) benefit from exceptions.

accidentTechnique.title: Technical breakdown — specifics
accidentTechnique.message: For temporary unemployment due to a technical breakdown, the benefit is paid only from the 8th calendar day. The first 7 days remain at the employer's expense.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.body:
Temporary unemployment (TU) is a provisional full or partial suspension of the employment contract, during which the worker can receive benefits paid by ONEM/RVA via their payment organisation.

Unlike full unemployment, the employment contract is not terminated — work resumes as soon as the cause of the suspension is gone.

Eleven official reasons exist, each with its own conditions, employer formalities and documents.

motifs.title: The eleven reasons
motifs.body:
The recognised reasons for temporary unemployment are:

'{{ motifs }}'

Each reason follows distinct admissibility rules and is only open to certain categories of workers (blue-collar, white-collar, temp worker).

quiEstConcerne.title: Who can claim which reason
quiEstConcerne.body:
Not all reasons are open to all statuses. The matrix below summarises eligibility.

'{{ qui-est-concerne }}'

A few special cases:

- **Economic**: a regular white-collar employee has no access — they switch to *White-collar suspension*, which requires the company to prove to ONEM/RVA that it meets the conditions (company in difficulty).
- **Medical force majeure**: does not apply to temp workers.
- **Protected worker**: reserved for union delegates (blue-collar or white-collar).
- **White-collar suspension**: white-collar only.

For temp workers in economic temporary unemployment, the situation remains tightly framed (required seniority, ongoing TU procedure for permanent workers in the same department, etc.).

conditions.title: Admissibility and award conditions
conditions.body:
Two levels of conditions apply:

1. **Admissibility** — the worker must have, over a reference period before the claim, enough proven workdays. Requirements vary by age and contract type.
2. **Award** — when the benefit is paid, several personal conditions must be met: availability for the labour market, no other replacement income, no sanction, etc.

A sanction or active exclusion on another basis (e.g. full unemployment) can directly affect temporary unemployment compensation.

documents.title: Documents to prepare
documents.body:
The documents that **the worker** submits in the context of a temporary unemployment claim:

'{{ documents }}'

In parallel, the **employer** sends ONEM/RVA their own declaration (DRS / WECH) electronically, along with the notifications and communications based on the reason. These flows do not pass through this file — they are handled directly by the employer through the ONSS/RSZ portals.

The C3.2 worker form is the cornerstone of the file in almost all cases. It can be replaced by other forms (C6) in specific situations like medical force majeure.

natureDa.title: Nature of the benefits claim (DA)
natureDa.body:
The « nature of the DA » is an ONEM/RVA code summarising the context of the claim (ongoing transfer, first claim ever, 66 years or older, applicable type of temporary unemployment, etc.).

It is derived from the answers to the orientation questions and the applicant's personal situation — it is not a question we ask the user, but a **computed value**.

The main natures encountered in TU:

- **TPL / TPV** — first claim (never had an unemployment file)
- **TFT** — TU transfer in progress
- **CTP** — 1st day of TU at 66 or older
- **INT** — blue-collar economic, white-collar suspension, bad weather
- **TEM** — technical breakdown, force majeure, medical force majeure, protected worker
- **VAC** — annual or supplementary leave

delais.title: Submission deadlines
delais.body:
The submission deadline depends on the claim type and reason:

| DA type | Standard deadline | Notable exceptions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Mandatory | current month + 2 months | Q/S change: + 36 months · Strike: + 6 months · 66+: no deadline |
| Optional | current month + 36 months | — |

Internal tools (AS400) do not always apply exceptions automatically and fall back to the shortest standard deadline — hence the importance, for the partner, of knowing the exact rule per reason.

demarchesEmployeur.title: Employer steps — overview
demarchesEmployeur.body:
On the employer side, obligations depend on the reason:

- **Notification** (preliminary procedure): required for economic, technical breakdown and white-collar suspension. It is done electronically before the start of the temporary unemployment period.
- **Communication of the 1st actual day**: to be sent each month for economic, bad weather, technical breakdown and white-collar suspension.
- **DRS / WECH**: electronic social declaration sent by the employer through the ONSS/RSZ portal. One concept, two names: *DRS* is the public name on the member side (Social Risk Declaration), *WECH* is the internal jargon (e.g. WECH 502 for the occurrence declaration, WECH 505 for the payment flow). For the member, these flows are invisible: they only see the result (benefits paid).

None of the employer formalities are part of the PDFs the worker fills in the file; we mention them here for information.

s1.title: Initialise the claim
s1.description: Create a new DA in the payment tool with the appropriate nature, the date of the 1st day of temporary unemployment, and the prefix matching the worker's situation (full-time, part-time, retention of rights, etc.).

s2.title: Determine the admissibility article
s2.description: Run the TU analysis to identify the applicable admissibility article. The system proposes the code (42K30, 42K33, 42L36, transitional measure, art. 42 after DISPO or not admissible).

s3.title: Request the ONSS / DRS flows
s3.description: Automatically claim the C3.2 EL (DRS flow) and, depending on the chosen admissibility article, the DMFA and any other relevant flow.

s4.title: Import the employment from the employer DRS
s4.description: Link the employment to the WECH 502 flow (claim) or WECH 505 (payment). Check consistency of imported parameters and feed the counters.

s5.title: Check the entitlement card (CAD)
s5.description: For a construction worker, check that a CAD exists. If so, select it. Otherwise the replacement allowance (€2) applies.

s6.title: Compute the numeric code
s6.description: Trigger the computation of the numeric code based on the new C32 form. For files whose admissibility relies on work (42K30 / 42K33), validate the TIMELINE to confirm the conditions are met.

s7.title: Finalise the benefits claim
s7.description: Set the claim status (complete / incomplete), position the C2 area, and let the program automatically compute the CAD code or the IC allowance depending on the sector.

s8.title: Validate the C2 coding
s8.description: Check the calculation method (08 — not subject to degressivity) and the scale — displayed automatically.

tem.title: TEM introduction — Force majeure & technical breakdown
tem.summary: Contract suspension due to force majeure (sudden and unforeseeable event), medical force majeure (temporary incapacity with insurance fund / occupational physician dispute) or technical breakdown. Covers blue-collar workers, white-collar workers and industrial apprentices.

delais.obligatoire: Current month + 2 months (end of the 2nd month after the DA)
delais.facultative: Current month + 36 months (end of the 36th month after the DA)
delais.exceptions: If the 1st day of the DA falls on a strike: mandatory deadline extended to 6 months

fmm.title: Attach the medical evidence
fmm.description: For medical force majeure: provide the decision of the occupational prevention adviser/physician (CPMT), the treating physician's opinion or proof of appeal against the insurance fund. Check job seeker registration within 8 days following the first 3 months.

accidentTechnique.title: Verify ONEM/RVA recognition
accidentTechnique.description: For a technical breakdown: ONEM/RVA recognition is mandatory. Compensation only runs from the 8th day of the event. The employer must communicate the 1st actual day of unemployment to ONEM/RVA (day before / day of / day after).

tem.notes: No job seeker registration required, EXCEPT for force majeure and medical force majeure (mandatory within 8 days following the first 3 months). Construction (JC 124): check the entitlement card (CAD) — right to the €2 allowance otherwise.

gre.title: GRE introduction — Strike / lock-out
gre.summary: Contract suspension during a strike (initiated by workers) or lock-out (initiated by the employer). Covers blue-collar workers, white-collar workers and industrial apprentices. Compensation depends on the decision of the ONEM/RVA management committee.

delais.obligatoire: Current month + 6 months (specific extended deadline for strikes)

decisionOnem.title: Trace the management committee decision
decisionOnem.description: Compensation depends on the ONEM/RVA management committee decision, reflected in the payment system by a flag: G (agreed — compensable), N (not agreed) or ? (awaiting response).

gre.notes: No job seeker registration. Economic TU admissibility conditions (codes 51 / 511) checked at each DA, regardless of the reason. Construction (JC 124): CAD check identical to TEM.

int.title: INT introduction — Bad weather, economic & white-collar suspension
int.summary: Three sub-cases: economic unemployment (blue-collar + industrial apprentices), bad weather (blue-collar + industrial apprentices) and white-collar suspension for lack of work. Employer notification / communication obligations differ per sub-case.

delais.exceptions: If 1st day = strike: mandatory extended to 6 months

economique.title: Notification + 1st day communication
economique.description: Economic: the employer notifies the planned period, must respect a week of resumption between two notifications (except small suspension), then communicates the 1st actual day to ONEM/RVA (same day, next business day, or within the 5 business days before).

intemperies.title: No notification, 1st day communication mandatory
intemperies.description: Bad weather: no prior notification, but mandatory communication to ONEM/RVA of the 1st actual day within the month (same day, next business day or previous business day).

suspensionEmployes.title: Notification period + nature, 1st day communication
suspensionEmployes.description: White-collar suspension: notification of the planned period and the nature of the interruption (complete / partial), then communication of the 1st actual day to ONEM/RVA. A single communication is enough if several forms of TU coexist in the month.

int.notes: No job seeker registration required. Construction (JC 124): CAD check identical to other natures.

ctp.title: CTP introduction — Worker past the legal retirement age
ctp.summary: Since 01/01/2015, a worker who continues past 65 can receive TU benefits (not cumulable with the pension). From the month following their 65th birthday and as soon as the first month in TU, a DA must be filed — mainly to restart the ONEM/RVA integration.

delais.obligatoire: Current month + 2 months (end of the 2nd month after the DA)
delais.facultative: Current month + 36 months (scenario 2); no deadline except prescription (scenario 3)

s1.title: Initialise the CTP
s1.description: Claim date = 1st of the month following the worker's 65th birthday. Nature = CTP. Prefix limited to 02 or 04 from 65 onwards (prefixes 05, 06, 57, 58 are no longer possible).

s2.title: Update member registry & restart integration
s2.description: Check the registry information, validate the integration. The usual integration ends at the latest on the last day of the month of the 65th birthday. The new integration then runs until the last day of the month in which the person turns 99 (code 15).

s3.title: Follow the full mandatory INT/TEM procedure
s3.description: Workflow identical to a mandatory INT DA (parameters + TU analysis + external documents + employment import + numeric code calculation + benefits + coding), plus printing the C1 from the household composition.

s4.title: Follow the optional INT/TEM procedure
s4.description: Workflow identical to an optional INT/TEM DA (copying the admissibility article, closing the period, employment import, recalculating the numeric code, benefits), plus printing the C1.

s5.title: Re-verify historical admissibility and finalise
s5.description: Check the admissibility article valid at the last TU DA. Copy article 042 + the admissibility into the parameters. Request the C3.2 EL and additional flows. Print the C1. Import the employment. Claim status = 1, C2 = Y. Reminder of the last valid coding.

s6.title: Validate the C2 coding
s6.description: Calculation method 08 (no degressivity) and single-line scale displayed automatically.

ctp.notes: Exception: medical force majeure remains accessible past 65. TU benefits cannot be cumulated with the pension. If the worker disputes a decision of the insurance fund's medical adviser, the DA is still filed normally with supporting evidence.

complet.title: Full unemployment
complet.description: Helps you build the full unemployment file after losing your job (dismissal, resignation, end of fixed-term contract) or for a first registration.

delaiInscription.title: Registration deadline: 8 days
delaiInscription.message: Register with the regional employment service (FOREM / ACTIRIS / VDAB / ADG) within 8 business days following the end of your contract. Late submission may result in loss of benefits for the period covered by the delay.

documentsEmployeur.title: Employer documents to request
documentsEmployeur.message: The C4 (unemployment certificate) must be given to you by your employer as soon as the contract ends. If it is delayed, request it in writing: without the C4, your payment organisation cannot build your file.

c4.title: C4 — Unemployment certificate (to be provided by the employer)

quiPeutBeneficier.title: Who can benefit?

delai8Jours.title: The 8-day deadline
delai8Jours.body:
The claim must be filed quickly after the end of the employment contract. In practice, a simple landmark applies: **8 business days** from the day following the end of the contract to register as a job seeker with the regional service and submit the file with the payment organisation (FGTB/ABVV, CSC/ACV, SYNOVA or CAPAC/HVW).

A late submission does not extinguish the right, but may cause loss of benefits for the period covered by the delay. In case of difficulty (illness, ongoing steps), the partner must be able to document the cause of the delay to assert it with ONEM/RVA.

Public reference: www.onem.be.

documentsAPreparer.title: The documents to prepare
documentsAPreparer.body:
The documents the worker prepares as part of a full unemployment claim:

'{{ documents }}'

Other official pieces are normally required but are not yet integrated into Beldoc — they must be added at a later stage:

- **C4** — Unemployment certificate given by the employer at the end of the contract. It certifies the duration of the work and the reason for the termination.
- **C61** — Control card, to be completed monthly by the unemployed person to declare activities and absences.
- **C109** — Family situation declaration, which determines the category (single / head of household / cohabitant) and therefore the amount of benefits.

For first unemployment registrations (young people after their studies), the regional employment service issues a registration attestation that opens the vocational integration period.

Public reference: www.onem.be.

frontalier.title: Cross-border unemployment
frontalier.description: For workers who live in Belgium but worked in a neighbouring EEA country, or vice versa. Helps you build the unemployment file in coordination with the foreign institution.

demandeU1.title: Request U1 — anticipate the delays
demandeU1.message: The U1 form must be requested from the unemployment institution of the country where you last worked. Allow several weeks. Without U1, ONEM/RVA cannot reconstruct your career abroad.

u2.title: U2 — to be requested before leaving
u2.message: If you want to look for a job in another EEA country while keeping your benefits, request U2 BEFORE leaving. Once on site, it is too late to open this right.

quiEstConcerne.title: Who is concerned?
quiEstConcerne.body:
« Cross-border unemployment » groups workers who live in a European Economic Area (EEA) country and work in another. Several profiles coexist:

- **Classic cross-border worker** — you live in one country and work in another, but you return home at least once a week.
- **Occasional cross-border worker** — same configuration, but you do not return every week (long assignment, accommodation on site…).
- **Posted worker** — your original employer temporarily sends you to work abroad (site, mission). You remain affiliated with the social security of your country of origin.

For Belgium, the most frequent configurations are BE/FR, BE/LU, BE/NL and BE/DE.

paysCompetent.title: Which country has jurisdiction?
paysCompetent.body:
The basic rule, set by the European regulation on social security coordination:

- **Full unemployment** (total loss of work) → jurisdiction lies with the **country of residence**. A Belgian cross-border worker in France who loses their job therefore turns to ONEM/RVA in Belgium.
- **Partial or temporary unemployment** (provisional contract suspension) → jurisdiction lies with the **country of the last employer**.

This rule has important consequences: the worker must know which institution to contact, and ONEM/RVA (or its foreign equivalent) must have proof of the employment periods completed in the other country. This is where the U1 form comes in.

formulaireU1.title: The U1 form — why is it crucial?
formulaireU1.body:
The **PD U1** (Portable Document U1) is issued by the unemployment institution of the country where the worker was employed. It certifies insurance and employment periods completed in that country.

Without U1, ONEM/RVA cannot reconstruct the career abroad to verify the admissibility conditions. In practice, this can block or delay the opening of the right to benefits in Belgium.

Two other European documents complement the system:

- **PD U2** authorises the temporary transfer of benefits to another EEA country while looking for a job there (limited to 3 months, extendable in some cases).
- **PD ED** is used for direct information exchange between social security institutions. The worker usually does not handle it — it is an administrative channel.

U1 must be requested from the competent foreign institution. Delivery times vary widely (from a few weeks to several months). It is therefore advisable to request it as soon as you know you will return to register for unemployment in your country of residence.

rcc.title: Unemployment scheme with company top-up (RCC/SWT)
rcc.description: Former pre-retirement: for workers at the end of their career, dismissed by their employer, who receive both unemployment benefits and a top-up paid by their former employer.

licenciementObligatoire.title: Dismissal mandatory
licenciementObligatoire.message: RCC/SWT IMPERATIVELY requires a dismissal by the employer. If you resign, you lose this right.

seuilsVariables.title: Variable thresholds
seuilsVariables.message: Age and seniority conditions change regularly (programme law, interprofessional agreement, sector-level CBAs). Check with your payment organisation the thresholds in force for your situation.

quEstCeQueLeRcc.title: What is RCC/SWT?
quEstCeQueLeRcc.body:
**RCC/SWT** (Unemployment scheme with company top-up) is the former « pre-retirement ». The name changed in 2012 but the logic remains the same: a worker at the end of their career who is dismissed receives **two combined incomes**:

1. The **unemployment benefits** paid by ONEM/RVA (like a regular unemployed person).
2. A **company top-up** paid by the former employer on top of the benefits. This top-up is not a favour: it stems from a collective bargaining agreement (sector- or company-level).

It is therefore not « early retirement » — the person remains officially unemployed until the statutory pension, with the associated obligations (except for a high-age exemption).

conditionsDeBase.title: Basic conditions
conditionsDeBase.body:
Four factors determine access to RCC/SWT. The numerical thresholds (62 years, 40 years of seniority, etc.) move along with interprofessional agreements and CBAs — they are given for indication and must be verified with the payment organisation for each file.

1. **Age** — usually 62 for the general scheme, 60 for heavy occupations. Lower thresholds exist in certain schemes (long career, company in difficulty).
2. **Career seniority** — typically 40 years in the general scheme, 33 years for heavy occupations, sometimes less depending on the CBA.
3. **Reason for contract end** — a **dismissal by the employer** is required. Resignation, mutual termination or voluntary departure close access to RCC/SWT.
4. **Applicable CBA** — there must be a collective agreement (sector- or company-level) that provides RCC/SWT for the worker's situation.

The file's orientation questions probe these factors:

'{{ questions }}'

No answer blocks the user; the answers serve to flag elements to check first with the payment organisation.

regimesSpeciaux.title: Special schemes
regimesSpeciaux.body:
Alongside the general scheme, several specific RCC/SWT schemes exist. They lower the age or seniority thresholds but impose their own conditions.

- **Heavy occupations** — night work, successive shifts (e.g. 3×8), interrupted work, physically demanding conditions. Usually grants access from 60 with 33 years of career (to be checked against the CBA).
- **Long career (40 years)** — for workers who accumulate 40 years of career, regardless of the « heavy » character of the job.
- **Company in difficulty** — the employer must be officially recognised as such by the Minister of Employment. Relaxed conditions for workers dismissed in that context.
- **Company in restructuring** — similar situation (recognised collective dismissal plan), with its own age and seniority conditions.

The applicable scheme is not a free choice of the worker: it depends on the CBA in force in the company or sector, and on the personal situation (age, career, profession). The union delegate and the payment organisation are the natural contacts to identify the right scheme.

insertion.title: Integration benefits (young people)
insertion.description: For young people leaving school who have not worked (enough) to open a right to classic unemployment benefits.

demandeAvant25.title: Claim before 25
demandeAvant25.message: The claim must in principle be filed BEFORE your 25th birthday. Past that age, the right to integration benefits is generally no longer open (except in special cases).

stageInsertion310j.title: 156-day vocational integration period
stageInsertion310j.message: You must first complete a 156-day vocational integration period (about 6 months) after the end of your studies before you can receive benefits.

questCeQue.title: What is the integration benefit?
questCeQue.body:
The integration benefit is an unemployment benefit for the young person who is leaving school and who, for the **first time**, claims compensation based on those studies — and not on prior salaried work.

It differs from classic full unemployment: there is no number of workdays to prove. The right rests on the school path and on completing a vocational integration period after the end of studies.

Public reference: www.onem.be.

conditions.title: The conditions (age, studies, 156-day period)
conditions.body:
Main conditions, paraphrased:

- **Nationality / residence**: be Belgian, or a national of a country that allows access (EU/EEA), or in good standing for residence and work in other situations.
- **End of compulsory schooling**: no longer subject to it (in practice: at least 18, or having passed the 30 June of the year of one's 18th).
- **Studies giving right to unemployment in Belgium**: completing a recognised study path. For young people **under 21**, a **diploma or qualification** is required (just attending classes is not enough) and the list of admitted studies is more restrictive.
- **Vocational integration period**: completing a **156-day** period (about 6 months) after the end of school activities.
- **Age limit**: the claim must in principle be filed **before 25** (exceptions exist, for example for a long study path).

Public reference: www.onem.be.

dureeEtMontant.title: How long / how much? (flat rate, limited to 1 year)
dureeEtMontant.body:
The amount of the integration benefit is **flat-rate**: it does not depend on a previous salary but on the young person's family situation (single, cohabitant, head of household) and, in some cases, on their age. There is no evolution by compensation periods like in classic unemployment.

The right is **time-limited**: since the reform effective 1 March 2026, 1 year (12 months) maximum (previously: 3 years). This duration may be extended by a period equal to any work periods or equivalent events during the benefit period.

Public reference: www.onem.be.

ADMINISTRATIVE_OFFICE.name: Administration & office
ADMINISTRATIVE_OFFICE.description: For organised people who enjoy paperwork, documents, case management and structured environments.

SOCIAL_CARE.name: Social & care work
SOCIAL_CARE.description: For people who want to support, listen to, help or guide others.

DIGITAL_IT.name: Digital & IT
DIGITAL_IT.description: For people drawn to computers, digital tools, the web, technical support or data work.

TECHNICAL_MANUAL.name: Technical & manual
TECHNICAL_MANUAL.description: For people who enjoy repairing, building, handling, producing or working with tools.

LOGISTICS_TRANSPORT.name: Logistics & transport
LOGISTICS_TRANSPORT.description: For people who like to move around, organise, prepare, deliver or manage flows.

SALES_CUSTOMER.name: Sales & customer relations

article-engager-premier-travailleur.title: Hiring your first employee
article-engager-premier-travailleur.summary: Hiring for the first time turns your business into an employer. A few social formalities must be in order before day one. Here are the main steps, jargon-free.
whatToKnow: From the first hire, your company gains "employer status" with the NSSO.
A Dimona declaration must be filed BEFORE the employee starts working.
You will then owe social contributions, declared quarterly through the DmfA.
Most employers register with an approved social secretariat that handles these formalities.
todo: Have your company identified as an employer with the NSSO (employer status).
Take out an occupational accident insurance (mandatory from the first employee).
File the Dimona declaration before the first day of work.
Check the applicable joint committee to know the minimum wage and working conditions.
Prepare a written employment contract when required or recommended.
commonMistakes: Letting the employee start before the Dimona has been submitted.
Forgetting the occupational accident insurance, mandatory from the very first hire.
Ignoring the joint committee and applying a wage below the scale.

article-faire-une-dimona.title: Filing a Dimona
article-faire-une-dimona.summary: Dimona is the immediate employment declaration: it notifies social security when an employee starts (and leaves). It is mandatory and must precede the start of work.
whatToKnow: Dimona = Immediate Declaration / Onmiddellijke Aangifte. It is filed online.
The entry declaration must be filed at the latest when the employee starts.
The type of worker (manual, salaried, student, flexi…) drives the code and required data.
An exit declaration is filed at the end of the contract.
todo: Sign in to the social security portal (or let your social secretariat do it).
Enter the employee's details and start date.
Validate the Dimona BEFORE the first day of work.
Keep the acknowledgement of receipt (Dimona reference number).
commonMistakes: Filing the Dimona late, after the start of work.
Choosing the wrong type of worker (impact on contributions).
Forgetting the exit Dimona at the end of the contract.

article-comprendre-dmfa.title: Understanding the DmfA
article-comprendre-dmfa.summary: The DmfA is the quarterly declaration that details remuneration, working time and social contributions for each employee. It is the basis for what you owe the NSSO.
whatToKnow: DmfA = multifunctional declaration, sent each quarter to the NSSO.
It lists, per employee, the remuneration, days/hours worked and contributions.
It serves several institutions (pensions, unemployment, healthcare): hence "multifunctional".
In practice, the social secretariat builds it from payroll data.
todo: Gather payroll and working-time data each quarter.
Send the data to your social secretariat (or enter it yourself).
Check consistency with the Dimonas and the pay slips.
Meet the quarterly deadline for filing and paying contributions.
documents: Record of days/hours worked and absences.
commonMistakes: Forgetting pay elements subject to contributions (bonuses, benefits).
Inconsistencies between Dimona, DmfA and pay slips.
Filing or paying after the quarterly deadline.

article-choisir-contrat-de-travail.title: Choosing the right employment contract
article-choisir-contrat-de-travail.summary: Open-ended, fixed-term, full- or part-time, student, flexi-job… The choice depends on the need and has social consequences. Some contracts require a mandatory written form.
whatToKnow: The open-ended contract is the default; fixed-term and special contracts have their own conditions and formalism.
Several contracts require a written, signed document BEFORE the start (part-time, student, flexi-job, agency work).
The contract states the job, the schedule, the place of work and the pay.
The joint committee sets minimum conditions that apply to the contract.
todo: Define the need (duration, hours, type of role).
Pick the appropriate contract type and check whether a written form is required.
Draft the contract with the mandatory clauses.
Have the contract signed before day one when a written form is required.
documents: Contract template suited to the chosen type.
Reference of the applicable joint committee and pay scale.
commonMistakes: Starting a part-time or student contract without a prior signed written form.
Omitting mandatory clauses (the working schedule, for example).
Chaining fixed-term contracts without a valid justification.

article-temps-partiel.title: Hiring part-time
article-temps-partiel.summary: Part-time work comes with specific rules: a written contract, a clear schedule and publication of variable schedules. A precise framework prevents disputes and adjustments.
whatToKnow: The part-time contract must be written and state the regime and working schedule.
For variable schedules, the slots and the way they are communicated must follow specific rules.
Schedules must be visible/consultable and the work rules must reflect them.
Part-time employees enjoy protections (e.g. priority for additional hours).
todo: Draft a written contract stating the working time and schedule.
Set a fixed schedule or a clear framework for variable schedules.
Align the work rules with the part-time schedules.
Organise the display / communication of schedules in line with the rules.
commonMistakes: Missing written form or an imprecise schedule in the part-time contract.
Failing to follow the rules on communicating variable schedules.
Schedules missing or inconsistent in the work rules.

article-etudiant.title: Hiring a student
article-etudiant.summary: Student work allows, under conditions, reduced contributions during an annual hours quota. The student employment contract is written and includes mandatory clauses.
whatToKnow: The student employment contract must be written and signed, with mandatory clauses.
An annual hours quota grants a reduced solidarity contribution.
The "student" Dimona is used to book and track hours under the quota.
Beyond the quota or without the conditions met, ordinary contributions apply.
todo: Draft a written student employment contract with all mandatory clauses.
File the "student" Dimona stating the hours, before the start.
Check the student's remaining quota balance.
Hand over the work rules and inform the student of their conditions.
documents: Tracking of hours under the quota.
commonMistakes: Letting the student work without a prior signed written contract.
Exceeding the quota without anticipating the switch to ordinary contributions.
Forgetting the student Dimona or misreporting the hours.

article-flexi-job.title: Hiring under flexi-job
article-flexi-job.summary: The flexi-job allows, in certain sectors and under conditions, hiring someone already sufficiently employed elsewhere or retired, under a specific social regime. A written framework agreement is required.
whatToKnow: Flexi-job assumes strict access conditions (sufficient employment elsewhere or retirement).
A written framework agreement is required, complemented by a per-period employment contract.
A specific employer contribution applies and the remuneration follows its own rules.
Flexi-job is open only in the sectors and situations set out by the regulation.
todo: Check that the employee meets the flexi-job access conditions.
Sign a written framework agreement, then a per-period employment contract.
File the specific flexi-job Dimona.
Apply the remuneration and contributions specific to the flexi regime.
documents: Evidence of the employee's access conditions.
commonMistakes: Hiring under flexi-job a person who does not meet the conditions.
Missing prior written framework agreement.
Applying the ordinary contribution regime instead of the flexi regime (or the other way around).

article-salaire-minimum-et-commission-pa.title: Minimum wage and joint committee
article-salaire-minimum-et-commission-pa.summary: The applicable minimum wage largely depends on the joint committee (JC) of the sector and on CBAs. Identifying your JC is a key step to set a compliant pay.
whatToKnow: Each activity falls under a joint committee that sets minimum conditions through CBAs.
The sectoral scale often prevails over the national interprofessional minimum.
Without knowing the JC, you cannot precisely verify the applicable scale.
Scales may depend on the role, seniority and age.
todo: Identify the joint committee matching your activity.
Consult the CBAs and scales applicable to the role.
Set the remuneration at least at the level of the sectoral scale.
Take seniority and any indexations into account.
documents: Reference of the company's joint committee.
commonMistakes: Applying the national minimum when a higher sectoral scale applies.
Failing to identify your joint committee.
Forgetting to index or to update the wage following the CBA.

article-reglement-de-travail.title: The work rules
article-reglement-de-travail.summary: The work rules set the internal rules (schedules, leave, pay terms, etc.). Putting them in place follows a procedure and they must be handed to every employee.
whatToKnow: Work rules are in principle mandatory as soon as you employ staff.
Their adoption and amendments follow a procedure (information, consultation, filing).
They contain mandatory clauses (schedules, payment of wages, leave…).
A copy must be handed to every employee and kept available.
todo: Draft the rules with the mandatory clauses.
Follow the introduction procedure (staff information, observation period).
File the rules with the competent authority.
Hand over a copy to every employee and keep proof.
documents: Proof of staff information and of filing.
commonMistakes: Failing to issue work rules while employing staff.
Amending the rules without following the procedure.
Forgetting to hand the rules to new employees.

article-duree-du-travail-et-repos.title: Working time and rest
article-duree-du-travail-et-repos.summary: Law sets the framework for working time, breaks and rest. Daily and weekly limits apply, with sectoral exceptions and rules for Sunday or night work.
whatToKnow: Daily and weekly working-time limits apply.
Rest (minimum interval, Sunday rest) and breaks are regulated.
Night work and Sunday work follow specific rules.
Exceptions and easings exist depending on the sector and the CBAs.
todo: Build schedules within daily/weekly limits.
Guarantee minimum rest and break times.
Check the specific rules for night/Sunday work if relevant.
Include the schedules in the work rules.
commonMistakes: Exceeding working-time limits without legal or CBA basis.
Failing to respect minimum rest times.
Letting employees work on Sundays/at night without checking the conditions.

article-chomage-temporaire.title: Resorting to temporary unemployment
article-chomage-temporaire.summary: Temporary unemployment suspends the contract in certain situations (force majeure, economic reasons, bad weather…). The procedure and documents vary by reason.
whatToKnow: Temporary unemployment suspends the contract without ending it, for a recognised reason.
Each reason (force majeure, economic, bad weather…) has its own conditions.
Notices to the NEO and documents for the employee are required depending on the reason.
The employee receives NEO benefits for the affected days.
todo: Check that the situation matches a recognised reason for temporary unemployment.
File the required notices/declarations with the NEO.
Inform the employees and hand them the necessary documents.
Keep track of the temporary unemployment days.
documents: Evidence of the reason (force majeure, economic, bad weather…).
Documents to hand over to the employee for their benefits.
commonMistakes: Invoking a reason that does not meet the legal conditions.
Skipping the mandatory notices to the NEO.
Failing to provide the employee with the documents needed for their benefits.

article-preparer-demande-secretariat-soc.title: Preparing your request to the social secretariat
article-preparer-demande-secretariat-soc.summary: The approved social secretariat handles payroll and social declarations. Preparing the information well upstream speeds up onboarding and makes the first payroll reliable.
whatToKnow: The social secretariat issues pay slips, the DmfA and often the Dimonas for you.
A clear onboarding and complete data prevent errors and adjustments.
You remain the responsible employer: the data you send must be accurate.
The more precise the joint committee and the contract, the more reliable the payroll.
todo: Gather the company's data (CBE, NSSO, accident insurance).
For each employee, prepare: contract, role, schedule, scale/JC.
Clarify the variable items (bonuses, expenses, benefits) to include in payroll.
Send a complete file and appoint a single point of contact.
documents: Joint committee and applicable pay scales.
commonMistakes: Sending incomplete data (missing JC, imprecise schedule).
Forgetting variable pay items.
Assuming that the social secretariat verifies everything on your behalf.

calendar.warning: Indicative dates calculated for a default quarterly regime. Deadlines may vary depending on your VAT regime, your employer situation, postponements to the next working day, public holidays, administrative tolerances or official communications. Always check the official sources from FPS Finance and Social Security.

ONSS_DMFA.label: NSSO declaration – DmfA
ONSS_DMFA.note: Quarterly regime (default). Deadline = last day of the month following the quarter.

PROFESSIONAL_WITHHOLDING_TAX.label: Professional withholding tax payment
PROFESSIONAL_WITHHOLDING_TAX.note: Default quarterly regime (the monthly regime is not modelled in MVP). Deadline = the 15th of the month following the quarter.

VAT_PERIODIC_RETURN.label: VAT return (quarterly)
VAT_PERIODIC_RETURN.note: Quarterly regime (default). Date taken from the official FPS calendar (weekend/holiday postponements already included).

calendar.fallbackNote: Calculated date (the 25th of the month following the quarter) because this quarter is missing from the official FPS calendar: the possible postponement to the next working day is not guaranteed, to be confirmed on the VAT calendar of FPS Finance.

assumption.employeeRate: White-collar: base employer contributions ≈ {pct} % of gross (occupational accident insurance and sector contributions on top, not included).

assumption.defaultRateUnknown: Worker type « unspecified »: default base employer rate ≈ {pct} % applied.

temps_partiel_sans_horaire.message: « Part-time » regime but no weekly schedule entered: the schedule is a mandatory mention in a part-time contract.
temps_partiel_sans_horaire.recommendation: Enter the weekly hours: a part-time contract must state the precise schedule.

net_incoherent.message: The net received entered ({net} €) differs sharply from the Docbel estimate (~{expected} € for a gross of {gross} €).
net_incoherent.recommendation: Check the deductions (NSSO, withholding) or whether unentered elements are at play (bonus, holiday pay, benefits, part-time). The estimate is indicative.

etudiant_sans_contrat.message: Student worker without contract data (neither JC nor schedule): the quota and solidarity contributions cannot be verified.
etudiant_sans_contrat.recommendation: Specify the student's schedule and JC to verify the annual quota and the solidarity contribution.

flexi_sans_contrat_cadre.message: Flexi-job worker without mention of a framework contract: a prior written framework contract is required for the flexi regime.
flexi_sans_contrat_cadre.recommendation: Confirm the existence of a signed flexi-job framework contract before work starts (mention it in the comment).

avantage_non_pris_en_compte.message: Benefits are declared but no amount (bonus, holiday pay) has been entered: they are not factored into the net estimate.
avantage_non_pris_en_compte.recommendation: Enter the amounts of the benefits/bonuses to refine the net comparison.

aucune_incoherence.recommendation: This check remains indicative: only an accredited social secretariat certifies the conformity of a payslip.

seo.title: eC3.2: interactive simulation to learn how to fill in your temporary unemployment control card | Docbel
seo.description: Understand how to complete the electronic temporary unemployment control card eC3.2 with an unofficial educational simulation, practical cases and common mistakes to avoid.

hero.title: eC3.2, simply explained
hero.subtitle: Test your electronic unemployment control steps in a clear, risk-free and 100% confidential simulator.
hero.disclaimer: No real data is requested. No data is transmitted to the NEO.

points.2: No data is transmitted to the NEO
points.3: This page does not replace the official eC3.2 application
points.4: To submit a real request, use the official eC3.2 application

alert.title: Before you start
alert.content: The eC3.2 card applies only to temporary unemployment (reduction or suspension of activity at your employer), not to be confused with the eC3, which applies to full unemployment. Everything shown here is fictitious and intended for learning only: no login, no submission and no real data is involved.

learningModes.title: How to learn
learningModes.subtitle: Three ways to approach the eC3.2 card, depending on whether you prefer to be guided, to practise on concrete cases, or to explore freely.

guided.title: Step-by-step guided mode
guided.description: Move forward step by step, from the simulated login to the fictitious submission. At each screen, an explanation and coach advice guide you.

scenarios.title: Practical cases
scenarios.description: Load a concrete case (work on an unemployment day, sickness, holidays, multiple employers...) directly into the simulator to practise.

free.title: Free exploration
free.description: Operate the calendar at your own pace: change the situations, test the corrections and observe what happens, without any constraints.

simulator.title: eC3.2 simulator — fictitious card
simulator.subtitle: An educational reconstruction of the application screens: login, declaration, choice of employer and month, filling the calendar, corrections, verification and simulated submission.
simulator.fictitiousDataNotice: The data shown is fictitious and intended for learning only.

login.title: Simulated login
login.description: Discover the identification methods (eID, itsme, cross-border workers) without any real login.

declaration.title: Sworn declaration
declaration.description: Choose your start month and confirm the switch to the electronic channel — fictitious step.

employer.title: Choice of employer
employer.description: Select the employer who placed you on temporary unemployment for the relevant month.

month.title: Choice of month
month.description: Open the card for the month to be filled; already-submitted cards are locked.

calendar.title: Fill in the calendar
calendar.description: Indicate your situations day by day: by default, each cell is considered as unemployment.

correction.title: Correct a mistake
correction.description: Modify a day already entered and explain the correction, as long as the card is not yet submitted.

verify.title: Verify
verify.description: Review all your entries before the simulated submission, especially the days worked.

send.title: Send
send.description: Confirm the fictitious submission of the card; no real data is transmitted.

coach.title: Docbel Coach
coach.intro: I guide you through each step with simple advice. Take your time: here you risk nothing, it is designed for learning.

tips.login: Choose an identification method to see how the login works. Nothing is requested here: no card, no code, no real login.
tips.declaration: The chosen month determines from when you use the eC3.2. If you were already on temporary unemployment before, you can activate an earlier month later.
tips.employer: Select the employer who placed you on temporary unemployment. Any other occupations you have will be indicated on the same card.
tips.month: Open the card for the month to be filled. An already-submitted card is locked: you can no longer modify it.
tips.calendar: By default, each day is considered as unemployment: you only indicate what changes (work, sickness, holidays...). All work must be recorded before starting.
tips.correction: To correct a day, clearly explain the change: this explanation helps inspectors. Only unsubmitted cards can be corrected.
tips.verify: Calmly re-read each day, especially the days worked and the incapacity days. Better to check twice than to submit an incomplete card.
tips.send: Check that the first possible submission date has been reached and that you are affiliated with a payment institution. Here, the submission is simulated: nothing goes to the NEO.

correctionModal.title: Correct the situation — simulation
correctionModal.lockedMessage: This card has been submitted and is locked in the simulation.
correctionModal.requiredError: An explanation is required to save a correction.

sendModal.title: Send the card — simulation
sendModal.body: In the real application, this step transmits the card to your payment institution. Here, no real data is sent.
sendModal.successBody: Your fictitious card is now locked.
sendModal.blockedBody: In the real application, you can fill in the card, but you must be affiliated with a payment institution to be able to send it.

pdf.fictionMention: Fictitious document — does not replace an official card.
pdf.warning: This document is not an official card.

resourcesSection.note: Docbel is not affiliated with the NEO. These links are provided for information.
